name: Chunk Sync - New Nodes (import into Zendesk Metrics - Kipu Sync)

parameters.query:
INSERT INTO public.sync_progress (
  run_id,
  flow_name,
  status,
  current_batch,
  total_batches,
  batch_size,
  updated_rows,
  note,
  updated_at
)
VALUES (
  '{{ (($json.run_id || "ticket-sync-run") + "").replace(/'/g, "''") }}',
  'ticket_sync',
  'running',
  0,
  {{ $json.total_chunks || 0 }},
  0,
  0,
  'Sync started: {{ ($json.date_from || "").replace(/'/g, "''") }} to {{ ($json.date_to || "").replace(/'/g, "''") }} — {{ $json.total_chunks || 0 }} chunks, ~{{ $json.total_count_estimate || 0 }} tickets',
  now()
)
ON CONFLICT (run_id) DO NOTHING;

meta.notes:
NEW CONNECTION MAP (wire these after importing nodes):
  Webhook - Run Sync → Code - Prep Count Query
  Code - Prep Count Query → HTTP - Count Full Range
  HTTP - Count Full Range → Code - Build Chunk List
  Code - Build Chunk List → Postgres - Init Sync Progress
  Postgres - Init Sync Progress → Split in Batches - Chunk Loop
  Split in Batches - Chunk Loop [output 0 loop] → Code - Prepare Run Sync Search (existing, MODIFIED)
  Split in Batches - Chunk Loop [output 1 done] → Code - Build Sync Response (existing)
  More Sync Pages? [output 1 false] → Wait - Between Chunks  (remove existing arrow to Code - Build Sync Response)
  Wait - Between Chunks → Split in Batches - Chunk Loop

NODES TO MODIFY (one at a time, next step):
  1. Code - Prepare Run Sync Search — read chunk from $json instead of Webhook - Run Sync body
  2. Postgres - Final Sync Count — UPDATE status=completed using global accumulated totals

ROOT CAUSE OF MISSING TICKETS:
  Zendesk search.json caps at 1,000 total results. Jan has 5,218 tickets so only ~1,000 were fetched.
  5-day chunks keep each chunk under 850 tickets, safely under the cap.
  Using created>= start and created< end eliminates the boundary-date gap where
  Base44 chunking silently dropped tickets on chunk boundary dates.
